rationale: Both candidates independently rediscovered the api_server.py hasattr-guard bug that silently skips the global UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES restriction, and both flagged the trust_remote_code default flip (True→False) as the main behavior-change concern, matching GT's core threads. Y additionally engages with the endpoint-restriction-resolves-wrong-pipeline thread (confirming the fix holds) and the hf_config caching/mutability concern raised by yuanheng-zhao, giving it broader recall of GT's discussion threads, while X's unique contributions (dead-code nit, missing UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES test) are more minor. X is slightly more actionable with explicit file:line/severity tags and concrete 'Fix:' instructions, but Y's narrative still ties findings to specific code and gives a clear verdict. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
